title: EscalationProposed payload
description: Emitted by the orchestrator-tier escalation contract when a trigger fires and the router proposes handing the next conversational turn from a cheap orchestrator-tier model (DeepSeek V4 Pro, Qwen) to a strong one (Opus 4.7) — or, on the down-edge, records a de-escalation. The proposal is advisory and recorded the instant the trigger fires; the actual model swap takes effect at the next turn boundary (docs/ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION.md §4). Entity is the orchestrator_session (slug = session_id), project-scoped when the harness supplies a project and cross-cutting otherwise. Actor is the substrate's system sentinel when emitted over the HTTP /mcp route (actor is transport-inferred, never caller-supplied); the meaningful model identities live in from_model/to_model, and the proposal's reason is recorded on the envelope rationale.

trigger.description: Which of the 5 closed trigger kinds fired. See docs/ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION.md §2 for the semantics of each.

from_model.description: Identifier of the cheap orchestrator model proposing escalation (e.g. deepseek-v4-pro, qwen2.5-32b). On a de-escalation edge this is the strong model handing control back. Payload-level, NOT the envelope actor.

to_model.description: Identifier of the model the next turn is proposed to run on. On an escalate edge this is the strong model (e.g. claude-opus-4-7); on a de-escalation edge it is the cheap model.

session_id.description: The orchestrator session the proposal belongs to. Also the entity slug, so a reader can fold every EscalationProposed for a session into its escalate/de-escalate arc.

turn_index.description: Which turn produced the proposal (0-based). Orders the proposals within a session.

state_after.description: Router state after the transition this event records. An escalate edge is cheap -> escalated; a de-escalation edge is escalated -> de_escalated. The pair carries the edge direction so de-escalation needs no separate event type.

trigger_detail.description: Free-form evidence the detector captured (retries used, observed confidence value, error kind). Human-readable; not parsed by consumers.

threshold_value.description: Snapshot of the threshold_value that fired, so a reader sees the contract's config at decision time without joining the (mutable) escalation_thresholds table. Null on de-escalation edges (no trigger).
